$comment: Rubric for the SKILL ITSELF, not for generated decks. Grounded in the skill-creator guidance and this repository's conventions. Defect-deduction scoring: each category starts at full points; deduct for concrete observed defects (minor=-1, major=-3, critical=-5 within the category, floor 0). Total = max 100. Judge = an independent reviewer given the full skill tree; every deduction must cite file:line evidence.
target: SKILL.md + references/ + scripts/ + evals/ + examples/ of this skill directory

categories.name: Trigger Design
criteria: The description explains both what the skill does and when to use it, including cases where the user does not say slide or pptx.
Boundaries with adjacent skills are explicit, with Not for cases that route to image slides, ATOM slides, narrative-only writing, Google Slides-only work, and financial models.
Trigger evals contain realistic prompts with paths, context, casual wording, and typos; should_fire and should_not_fire each have at least eight cases.
should_not_fire cases are near misses, not unrelated filler.

categories.name: Progressive Disclosure And Structure
criteria: SKILL.md stays under 500 lines and separates metadata, operating workflow, and reference map.
Each reference file has a clear role and is read only when relevant.
Long reference files are organized with headings.
scripts, references, evals, examples, and assets have distinct responsibilities.

categories.name: Instruction Quality
criteria: Instructions are imperative and explain the why behind major constraints.
Guidance is generalized for diverse future deck work and is not overfit to one sample deck.
Output formats and examples are concrete enough to execute.
Redundant, dead, contradictory, or unreferenced guidance is absent.

categories.name: Determinism And Verification
criteria: Binary generation, rendering, validation, and linting live in scripts; the LLM handles judgment and content.
Machine checks and visual/subjective checks are separated and both are required.
Automated tests cover scripts and key behavioral contracts.
Evals have objective assertions and a quality rubric with a pass threshold.

categories.name: Workflow Completeness
criteria: The phases are explicit: requirements, outline gate, generation, validation, repair loop, and final report.
Intermediate artifacts are files so work can resume across sessions.
The repair menu defines local fixes and when to return upstream.

categories.name: Operability And Safety
criteria: Dependencies, external CLIs, and rendering prerequisites are documented.
The skill follows the user's intent without surprising destructive or hidden behavior.
The tree is compatible with repo validation and avoids cache artifacts.
